assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements prepared under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend is denominated in a foreign currency and a corresponding intragroup receivable has been recognised.
The issue is the possible hedge accounting model for foreign exchange risk in consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities and components of them that could affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.
